strengths: Demonstrated proper traction setup with Maryland forceps positioning
Maintained steady scissor control during the cutting phases
Successfully followed the circle path for most of the circumference
weaknesses: Failed to complete the full circle within the time limit
Inconsistent cutting pace led to timeout before task completion
May have lost focus on maintaining continuous progress around the circle

drills.name: Circle tracking quarter-cuts
drills.description: Cut the circle in four controlled quadrants while maintaining traction, focusing on steady pace throughout each segment.
drills.targets_weakness: Failed to complete the full circle within the time limit.

drills.name: Traction-angle practice
drills.description: 10 repetitions of repositioning the Maryland to improve scissor entry angle and maintain optimal cutting efficiency.
drills.targets_weakness: Inconsistent cutting pace led to timeout before task completion.

progression_note: At the intermediate level, focus on converting your solid technique into consistent pacing to ensure task completion within time limits. Your cutting mechanics are sound, but maintaining steady progress throughout the entire circle is crucial for advancing to the next level.
